title: ISO 9001 Overview
description: An internationally recognized standard for quality management systems.

questions.question: What is the primary focus of ISO 9001?
questions.explanation: ISO 9001 focuses on quality management systems to ensure consistent customer satisfaction.

questions.question: What does PDCA stand for?
questions.explanation: PDCA (Plan-Do-Check-Act) is the continuous improvement cycle in ISO 9001.

questions.question: Which is a key principle of ISO 9001?
questions.explanation: Customer focus is one of the seven quality management principles.

questions.question: What is the purpose of internal audits?
options: To ensure compliance with policies and procedures
questions.explanation: Internal audits verify QMS compliance and identify improvement opportunities.

questions.question: What does continual improvement mean?
questions.explanation: Continual improvement is recurring activity to enhance performance.

title: ISO 27001 Intro
description: An international standard that provides a framework for information security management systems or ISMS.

questions.question: What is the main purpose of ISO 27001?
questions.explanation: ISO 27001 provides a framework for Information Security Management Systems.

questions.question: What are the three pillars of information security?
questions.explanation: The CIA triad is the foundation of information security.

questions.question: What is risk assessment in ISO 27001?
questions.explanation: Risk assessment identifies threats, vulnerabilities, and determines appropriate controls.

questions.question: What is an information asset?
questions.explanation: Information assets include data, hardware, software, people, and facilities.

questions.question: How often should ISMS be reviewed?
questions.explanation: ISO 27001 requires management reviews at planned intervals.

title: Quality and Information Security Policy
description: Learn how quality management and information security work together.

questions.question: Why integrate ISO 9001 and ISO 27001?
questions.explanation: Integration creates synergies and improves efficiency.

questions.question: What is common to both standards?
questions.explanation: Both standards share risk-based thinking and PDCA cycle.

questions.question: How are data quality and security related?
options: Poor quality affects security and vice versa
questions.explanation: Data quality and security are interconnected and interdependent.

questions.question: Who is responsible for quality and security?
questions.explanation: Quality and security are everyone's responsibility.

questions.question: What is a benefit of documented procedures?
questions.explanation: Documented procedures ensure consistency and reduce errors.

title: Internet Acceptable Use Policy
description: Understand acceptable use guidelines for internet access at work.

questions.question: What is the purpose of Internet Acceptable Use Policy?
options: Define acceptable use and protect resources
questions.explanation: The policy establishes guidelines for safe and appropriate internet use.

questions.question: Which is typically acceptable internet use?
options: Sharing confidential info on social media
questions.explanation: Acceptable use includes legitimate business activities.

questions.question: Why avoid suspicious links?
questions.explanation: Suspicious links are common vectors for cyber attacks.

questions.question: What if you accidentally access restricted content?
options: Close immediately and report to IT
questions.explanation: Transparency protects you and the organization.

questions.question: Is brief personal internet use allowed?
questions.explanation: Personal use must align with company policy.

title: Malicious Code Awareness
description: Equips you with knowledge to protect digital assets through understanding malicious code and preventive actions.

questions.question: What is malware?
options: Malicious software designed to harm systems
questions.explanation: Malware is software intentionally designed to cause damage.

questions.question: Which is a type of malware?
questions.explanation: Ransomware encrypts data and demands payment.

questions.question: How often should antivirus definitions update?
questions.explanation: Daily updates protect against new threats.

questions.question: What should you do if antivirus detects malware?
options: Follow prompts and report to IT
questions.explanation: Follow antivirus prompts and notify IT immediately.

questions.question: Can malware spread through email?
questions.explanation: Email is a common malware delivery method.

title: Access Control Policy
description: This policy ensures that access to information is controlled based on business and security requirements.

questions.question: What is the principle of least privilege?
options: Users get only access they need
questions.explanation: Least privilege minimizes security risks by limiting access.

questions.question: What is multi-factor authentication?
questions.explanation: MFA requires two or more verification factors.

questions.question: Should you share your credentials?
questions.explanation: Credentials are personal and must never be shared.

questions.question: When should access rights be reviewed?
questions.explanation: Regular reviews ensure appropriate access levels.

questions.question: What is role-based access control?
questions.explanation: RBAC grants access based on organizational roles.

title: Network Access Policy
description: Learn about secure network access and connection guidelines.

questions.question: Why is network segmentation important?
questions.explanation: Segmentation contains breaches and limits lateral movement.

questions.question: Is public WiFi safe for work?
questions.explanation: Public WiFi is insecure; use VPN for protection.

questions.question: What is a VPN?
questions.explanation: VPN encrypts traffic over untrusted networks.

questions.question: Should you connect personal devices to company network?
questions.explanation: Personal devices must meet security requirements.

questions.question: What is a firewall?
options: An embedded software used for blocking unwanted apps
A form of antivirus that can be controlled
questions.explanation: Firewalls monitor and control network traffic.

title: Data Protection & Privacy Policy
description: Understand data protection principles and privacy regulations.

questions.question: What is personal data?
options: Any information about you that cannot be shared
questions.explanation: Personal data is any information relating to an identifiable person.

questions.question: What is GDPR?
questions.explanation: GDPR is EU regulation protecting personal data.

questions.question: What is data minimization?
options: Collect data based on given resources
Collect limited data for a limited time
Removal of unnecessary data for performance
questions.explanation: Data minimization limits collection to what is needed.

questions.question: Can you share customer data freely?
questions.explanation: Customer data requires proper authorization and controls.

questions.question: What is a data breach?
questions.explanation: Data breach is unauthorized access, disclosure, or loss.

title: Cryptographic Control Policy
description: Learn about encryption and cryptographic controls.

questions.question: What is encryption?
questions.explanation: Encryption protects data by making it unreadable without the key.

questions.question: When should data be encrypted?
questions.explanation: Encryption protects data both during transmission and storage.

questions.question: What is a cryptographic key?
options: A string of characters used to encrypt/decrypt data
A program to decipher encrypted data
An application used for encrypting user info
A security protocol for safekeeping data
questions.explanation: Cryptographic keys are secrets used in encryption algorithms.

questions.question: Should encryption keys be shared?
options: Yes, it is not illegal to do so
questions.explanation: Keys must be protected and never shared insecurely.

questions.question: What is SSL/TLS?
questions.explanation: SSL/TLS encrypts web traffic (HTTPS).

title: Cloud Service Policy
description: This policy ensures proper management and security of cloud services.

questions.question: What is cloud computing?
options: An advanced computing method for fast network communication
questions.explanation: Cloud computing delivers services via internet.

questions.question: Can you use any cloud service for work?
questions.explanation: Only approved cloud services meet security requirements.

questions.question: What is Shadow IT?
questions.explanation: Shadow IT is unapproved technology use that creates risks.

questions.question: Who is responsible for cloud data security?
questions.explanation: Cloud security is a shared responsibility model.

questions.question: Should company data be stored in personal cloud accounts?
questions.explanation: Company data requires approved business-grade cloud services.

title: Data Leak Prevention
description: Learn how to prevent unauthorized data disclosure.

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of Data Leak Prevention?
options: Creating strategies to block unauthorized transmission
To monitor user access in handling data
questions.explanation: DLP prevents sensitive data from leaving the organization.

questions.question: Which is a data leak risk?
options: Sending confidential files using personal emails
Connecting external devices to the network
Taking photos of the office environment
questions.explanation: Personal email is an unauthorized channel for company data.

questions.question: What should you do before emailing sensitive data?
options: Notify the recipient and share decryption keys
Verify the recipient and encrypt when needed
Use BCC and send within company grounds
questions.explanation: Always verify recipients and protect sensitive data.

questions.question: Are screenshots of confidential data allowed?
questions.explanation: Screenshots must follow same confidentiality rules.

questions.question: What is data classification?
questions.explanation: Classification determines appropriate handling controls.

title: Secure Coding Policy
description: Understand secure software development practices.

questions.question: What is secure coding?
questions.explanation: Secure coding prevents security vulnerabilities in software.

questions.question: What is an injection attack?
options: Inserting malicious code to allow entry
Inserting external drives to gain unauthorized access
questions.explanation: SQL injection exploits poor input validation.

questions.question: Should passwords be hardcoded in source code?
questions.explanation: Hardcoded credentials are a critical security flaw.

questions.question: What is input validation?
options: Checking user input for proper format
Checking user input for malicious content
questions.explanation: Input validation prevents injection attacks.

questions.question: When should code be reviewed for security?
options: Security is not part of code reviews
questions.explanation: Security reviews should be integrated throughout development.

title: Risk Assessment Procedure
description: Learn how to identify and assess information security risks.

questions.question: What is risk assessment?
options: The identification and evaluation of threats
A form of survey identifying risks
questions.explanation: Risk assessment identifies threats and vulnerabilities.

questions.question: What is considered as a threat?
questions.explanation: Threats are potential sources of harm.

questions.question: What is a vulnerability?
options: A weakness that can be exploited
A mitigated attack within the network
questions.explanation: Vulnerabilities are weaknesses in systems or processes.

questions.question: How is risk calculated?
questions.explanation: Risk = Likelihood of occurrence × Impact of consequences.

questions.question: What is risk treatment?
questions.explanation: Risk treatment includes mitigation, transfer, acceptance, or avoidance.

title: Document Control Awareness
description: Understand document management and version control.

questions.question: Why is document control important?
options: To lessen paperwork and use of physical paper
To control information passed through documents
questions.explanation: Document control ensures accuracy, traceability and compliance with the latest authorized version.

questions.question: What is version control?
questions.explanation: Version control tracks changes and maintains history.

questions.question: Should you work from local copies of controlled documents?
questions.explanation: Local copies may be outdated; use official versions.

questions.question: What is document retention?
options: Keeping documents for a required period
Deleting documents after a required period
Archiving documents after a certain period
Deciding which documents are allowed to be thrown
questions.explanation: Retention ensures documents are kept as legally required.

questions.question: Who can approve controlled documents?
questions.explanation: Only authorized individuals can approve changes.

title: External Parties
description: The external parties policy aims to maintain the security of our information processing facilities and assets.

questions.question: What should you do before sharing sensitive information?
options: Let a higher up check the content
Make a copy for backup purposes
questions.explanation: Always verify recipients have authorization for sensitive data.

questions.question: Which method is secure for sharing confidential files?
options: Encrypted file transfer or secure portal
questions.explanation: Use approved secure channels for confidential information.

questions.question: Which of the following is not part of an external party?
questions.explanation: External Party categories include agreements and correct information exchange under proper procedure.

questions.question: What is an NDA?
questions.explanation: NDAs legally protect confidential information.

questions.question: Can you forward confidential emails?
options: Yes, but only within your department
questions.explanation: Forwarding requires same authorization as original sharing.

title: Asset Inventory
description: The asset inventory procedure ensures that we asset ownership, documentation, and location, crucial for security and efficiency.

questions.question: Why maintain an asset inventory?
questions.explanation: Asset inventory enables proper security and management.

questions.question: What is an information asset?
questions.explanation: Information assets include all resources supporting information processing.

questions.question: Should personal devices be in the inventory?
questions.explanation: Work-used personal devices (BYOD) require tracking.

questions.question: What is asset classification?
questions.explanation: Classification determines appropriate protection levels.

questions.question: When should inventory be updated?
options: When assets are added, changed, or removed
questions.explanation: Inventory must reflect current state of assets.

title: Email Rules
description: Learn proper email usage and security practices.

questions.question: What is phishing?
options: Fraudulent emails attempting to steal information
questions.explanation: Phishing uses deceptive emails to trick victims.

questions.question: How do you identify phishing emails?
options: Only spam is considered as phishing
questions.explanation: Phishing shows red flags like urgency and suspicious links.

questions.question: Should you click links in unexpected emails?
options: Yes, if the company is known
No, always verify the sender first
questions.explanation: Always verify unexpected emails before clicking.

questions.question: Is email suitable for highly confidential information?
questions.explanation: Standard email is not secure enough for highly confidential data.

questions.question: What should you do with suspicious emails?
questions.explanation: Report phishing attempts to help protect others.

title: Information Asset Classifications
description: The process of categorizing information based on sensitivity and importance.

questions.question: What is classification?
options: categorized information based on essence over time
categorized information based on shareholder value
categorized information based on sensitivity and importance
questions.explanation: Classification is the process of categorizing information based on sensitivity and importance that helps protect data effectively.

questions.question: Which of the following does not belong to the key considerations of classification?
questions.explanation: The key considerations (legality, value, sensitivity, criticality) are taken into account when classifying assets.

questions.question: They primarily Classify, Label, and Handle the classification system.
questions.explanation: Asset owners are responsible for classifying, labeling, and handling information correctly according to its classification.

questions.question: Which of the following does not belong?
questions.explanation: Removing confidential information requires approval and safeguards.

questions.question: What if you accidentally disclose confidential info?
questions.explanation: Immediate reporting enables damage control.

title: Teleworker Policy
description: Understand secure remote work practices.

questions.question: What is most important when working remotely?
options: Using faster and better personal devices
questions.explanation: Remote work requires same security as office work.

questions.question: Should you use public WiFi for work?
options: No, or use VPN if necessary
questions.explanation: Public WiFi is insecure without VPN protection.

questions.question: How to secure your home workspace?
options: Lock devices, use privacy screens, secure network
questions.explanation: Home workspace requires physical and digital security.

questions.question: Can family members use your work computer?
options: No, work devices are for authorized use only
questions.explanation: Work devices must be used only for authorized purposes.

questions.question: What if your work device is lost or stolen?
questions.explanation: Immediate reporting enables remote wipe and damage control.

title: Reporting InfoSec Events
description: Learn how to identify and report security incidents.

questions.question: What is a security incident?
questions.explanation: Security incidents are events that breach or threaten security.

questions.question: When should you report a suspected incident?
questions.explanation: Quick reporting enables faster response and limits damage.

questions.question: What is NOT a security incident?
questions.explanation: Scheduled maintenance is planned, not an incident.

questions.question: Who should handle security incidents?
questions.explanation: Security incidents require specialized response team.

questions.question: What should you do if you suspect compromise?
options: Continue working and report it at the end of the day
questions.explanation: Proper incident response includes containment and preservation.

title: Training Policy
description: Understand the importance of security awareness training.

questions.question: Why is security training important?
options: To build awareness and reduce risk
questions.explanation: Training empowers employees to protect information.

questions.question: How often should security training occur?
options: Yearly training is a good standard
questions.explanation: Ongoing training maintains awareness of evolving threats.

questions.question: Who needs security training?
questions.explanation: Everyone plays a role in information security.

questions.question: What topics should training cover?
options: Standards, policies, threats, and best practices
Proper resource use and workplace etiquette
questions.explanation: Comprehensive training covers all relevant security topics.

questions.question: Is completing training enough?
options: Yes, awareness is good enough for the organization
Yes, training is enough for compliance purposes
No, employees must apply knowledge every work day
No, it needs certification to further test knowledge
questions.explanation: Training effectiveness requires practical application.

title: Physical Entry Controls
description: Learn about physical security and access controls.

questions.question: What is a secured area?
options: Restricted area requiring ID to enter
questions.explanation: Secured areas contain sensitive assets or information.

questions.question: Should you hold doors open for others?
options: No, everyone must use own access
questions.explanation: Tailgating allows unauthorized access; everyone must authenticate.

questions.question: What should you do with your access credentials?
options: Lend to visitors and new hires
questions.explanation: Access badges are personal and must not be shared.

questions.question: How should visitors be handled?
options: Give access to all areas temporarily
questions.explanation: Visitors require escorting to prevent unauthorized access.

questions.question: What if you find an unlocked secured area?
questions.explanation: Security breaches must be reported for investigation.

title: Disposal Policy
description: Learn proper methods for disposing of sensitive information.

questions.question: Why is secure disposal important?
options: Reduces clutter and make space for more important resources
Recycles resources to avoid further costs
Saves storage for future company use
Prevents data recovery by unauthorized parties
questions.explanation: Improper disposal can lead to data breaches.

questions.question: How should confidential papers be disposed?
questions.explanation: Cross-cut shredding makes document reconstruction impossible.

questions.question: How to dispose of old hard drives?
options: Format and Donate to other organizations
Format and re-sell it to other parties
Degaussing, physical destruction, or certified wiping
Keep it and reuse for future storage
questions.explanation: Hard drives require certified data destruction methods.

questions.question: Can flash drives be reused after deleting files?
options: No, must be securely wiped or destroyed
questions.explanation: Deleted files can be recovered without secure wiping.

questions.question: What about expired IDs?
questions.explanation: Old badges could be misused for unauthorized access.

title: Preventive Maintenance
description: Understand the importance of system maintenance for security.

questions.question: Why is preventive maintenance important for security?
options: Saves cost by avoiding regular maintenance
Follows a schedule for security compliance
questions.explanation: Well-maintained systems are more secure and reliable.

questions.question: What is patch management?
questions.explanation: Patches fix security vulnerabilities in software.

questions.question: How often should systems be updated?
questions.explanation: Regular updates protect against known vulnerabilities.

questions.question: Should you delay security patches?
questions.explanation: Delays leave systems vulnerable to known exploits.

questions.question: What is vulnerability scanning?
options: An example is an antivirus scan
questions.explanation: Scanning proactively identifies security issues.

title: Backup: Protecting Information Assets
description: Learn about data backup and recovery procedures.

questions.question: Why are backups critical?
options: To have extra storage for future use
To create multiple copies of software
To enable recovery from data loss
To have various data access points
questions.explanation: Backups protect against data loss from various causes.

questions.question: What is the 3-2-1 backup rule?
questions.explanation: 3-2-1 rule ensures backup redundancy and availability.

questions.question: How often should critical data be backed up?
questions.explanation: Critical data requires frequent backups to minimize loss.

questions.question: Should backups be tested?
questions.explanation: Untested backups may fail when needed.

questions.question: Where should backup media be stored?
questions.explanation: Offsite storage protects against site-wide disasters.

title: Control of Operational Software
description: Understand software licensing and authorized usage.
badgeDescription: Finished training the Software Regulation policy

questions.question: Why must software be approved before installation?
options: To ensure security, licensing, and compatibility
questions.explanation: Unapproved software poses security and legal risks.

questions.question: Is pirated software allowed?
options: Yes, if allowed by your manager
questions.explanation: Pirated software is illegal and may contain malware.

questions.question: What is software licensing?
questions.explanation: Licenses define legal terms for software usage.

questions.question: Can you share licensed software with others?
questions.explanation: Each user/device typically requires separate license.

questions.question: What is open source software?
options: Software rights that can be bought
Unifinished software that anybody can finish
questions.explanation: Open source allows code inspection and modification.

title: Time Synchronization
description: Learn the importance of accurate time across systems.

questions.question: Why is time synchronization critical?
questions.explanation: Accurate time is essential for security event correlation.

questions.question: What does NTP stand for?
questions.explanation: NTP synchronizes computer clocks over networks.

questions.question: What happens with unsynchronized clocks?
questions.explanation: Time discrepancies hinder incident investigation.

questions.question: What is stratum in NTP?
questions.explanation: Stratum indicates distance from reference clock.

questions.question: How does time sync support security?
questions.explanation: Accurate timestamps are crucial for forensics and compliance.

title: Username & Password Management
description: Learn best practices for credential management.

questions.question: What makes a strong password?
questions.explanation: Strong passwords are at least 12 characters with mixed character types.

questions.question: Should you reuse passwords?
options: Yes, it is good practice to do so
Yes, so it's easier to remember
No, use unique passwords for each system
questions.explanation: Password reuse allows compromise to spread across accounts.

questions.question: What is a password manager?
options: A tool to securely store passwords
A security role for handling passwords
questions.explanation: Password managers encrypt and manage credentials securely.

questions.question: How often should passwords be changed?
questions.explanation: Regular changes without cause may weaken security through predictable patterns.

questions.question: What is multi-factor authentication?
questions.explanation: MFA requires something you know, have, and/or are.
